Why I Built a Personal Website
A few thoughts on why having your own corner of the internet still matters in the age of social media.

In a world dominated by social platforms, having your own website might seem old-fashioned. I think it's more important than ever.

## Ownership

When you publish on someone else's platform, you're a guest. Algorithms change, accounts get suspended, and platforms shut down. Your own site is **yours**.

## Simplicity

A static site is:

- **Fast** — no server-side rendering, no database queries
- **Secure** — no attack surface beyond static files
- **Cheap** — free hosting on GitHub Pages, Netlify, or Cloudflare Pages
- **Durable** — HTML files work everywhere, forever

## A Creative Outlet

Building and maintaining a personal site is a small creative project that compounds over time. Every post, every tweak to the design — it all adds up.

If you've been thinking about making your own site, just start. It doesn't have to be perfect.

Learn how this site was built using Astro, a modern static site generator that ships zero JavaScript by default.

Welcome to my blog! This is the first post on my new Astro-powered website.

## Why Astro?

Astro is a modern static site generator that focuses on delivering fast websites by shipping **zero JavaScript** to the browser by default. It supports Markdown content out of the box, making it perfect for blogs.

### Key Features

- **Content Collections** — Type-safe Markdown with Zod schema validation
- **File-based Routing** — Pages are just files in the `src/pages/` directory
- **Component Islands** — Add interactivity only where you need it
- **Fast by Default** — No JS shipped unless you opt in

## What's Next?

I'll be sharing more posts about web development, tooling, and things I learn along the way. Stay tuned!
